description: Plan 4 — Source Connectors + Embedding Pipeline. Wire up pgvector, Google Drive connector, GitHub connector, chunking/embedding pipeline, and tenant-safe semantic retrieval. Stops at retrieval — no drafting.

userStories.title: Add pgvector extension and knowledge tables migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the vector database infrastructure and knowledge tables in place before any embedding can be stored.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable pgvector extension in Alembic migration 0004_plan4_connectors.py

source_connectors table: workspace_id (FK workspaces.id CASCADE), connector_type (VARCHAR 32, enum: notion|google_drive|github), config (JSONB), encrypted_credentials (BYTEA), encrypted_credentials_nonce (BYTEA 12), sync_status (VARCHAR 32, default 'not_synced'), last_synced_at (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable), disconnected_at (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ server_default now())

source_documents table: workspace_id (FK), connector_id (FK source_connectors.id CASCADE), external_id (TEXT), title (TEXT), url (TEXT nullable), content_hash (VARCHAR 64), provider_updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable), last_synced_at (TIMESTAMPTZ nullable); UNIQUE(workspace_id, connector_id, external_id)

knowledge_chunks table: workspace_id (FK), source_document_id (UUID FK nullable), knowledge_entry_id (UUID nullable), chunk_index (INT), content (TEXT), embedding (vector(1536)), embedding_model (VARCHAR 64), embedding_dims (INT), content_hash (VARCHAR 64), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ); ivfflat index on embedding with lists=100
retrieval_logs table: id (UUID PK), workspace_id (FK), draft_id (UUID nullable), sources_used (JSONB), query (TEXT), retrieved_at (TIMESTAMPTZ server_default now())
RLS ENABLE on all four tables; policy pattern: USING (workspace_id::text = current_setting('app.current_workspace_id', true))
ORM models SourceConnector, SourceDocument, KnowledgeChunk, RetrievalLog added to relay/db/models.py with correct relationships
alembic upgrade head runs without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Install pgvector: add pgvector to pyproject.toml dependencies. Use sqlalchemy_utils or raw DDL for the vector column type. The ivfflat index must use CREATE INDEX ... USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists=100). RLS policy mirrors the pattern in 0001_initial_schema.py.

userStories.title: Connector interface, registry, and ConnectorType enum
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a normalized Connector abstract base class and factory so all connector types share one interface.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/__init__.py created

relay/connectors/base.py defines abstract class Connector with async methods: sync(workspace_id: uuid.UUID) -> None, search(workspace_id: uuid.UUID, query: str, top_k: int) -> list, citation(chunk) -> dict, disconnect(workspace_id: uuid.UUID) -> None, purge(workspace_id: uuid.UUID) -> None
relay/connectors/registry.py defines get_connector(connector_type: str) -> Connector that returns the correct class instance; raises ValueError for unknown types
ConnectorType string enum added to relay/db/models.py: google_drive, github (notion reserved for post-launch)
Unit tests in tests/test_connector_registry.py: valid types return correct class, invalid type raises ValueError
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Python abc.ABC and abc.abstractmethod. The registry maps string type names to classes (not instances). Do not import connector implementations at module level — use lazy imports inside get_connector() to avoid circular imports.

userStories.title: Shared embedding pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a shared async embed_chunks() function that connectors call to turn text chunks into vectors stored in knowledge_chunks.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/embeddings.py implements async embed_chunks(workspace_id, chunks: list[str], connector_id: uuid.UUID | None, source_document_id: uuid.UUID | None, session: AsyncSession) -> list[uuid.UUID]
Uses EMBEDDING_PROVIDER env var (voyage|openai, default voyage); import the correct client based on the env var
For Voyage: use voyageai Python client, model voyage-3 (or voyage-3-lite), batch size 20
For OpenAI: use openai Python client, model text-embedding-3-small (1536 dims), batch size 20
Stores KnowledgeChunk rows: embedding_model, embedding_dims, content_hash (sha256 of content), chunk_index
Idempotent: skip re-embedding if a KnowledgeChunk with the same content_hash already exists for this workspace
Unit tests in tests/test_embeddings.py: mock embedding API, verify chunk rows created, verify idempotency (same hash → no duplicate row)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: content_hash = hashlib.sha256(content.encode()).hexdigest(). Add voyageai and/or openai to pyproject.toml as optional dependencies. The EMBEDDING_PROVIDER defaults to voyage. chunk_index is the position within the document (0-indexed).

userStories.title: Shared chunking utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a chunk_text() function that splits document text into overlapping chunks suitable for embedding.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/chunking.py implements chunk_text(text: str, max_tokens: int = 800, overlap_tokens: int = 100) -> list[str]
Uses tiktoken with cl100k_base encoding for token counting
Chunks respect token boundaries (never cuts mid-token)
Last chunk may be shorter than max_tokens
Empty or whitespace-only input returns empty list
Unit tests in tests/test_chunking.py: empty input, single chunk (short text), multi-chunk, overlap boundary, exact max_tokens boundary
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add tiktoken to pyproject.toml. Use enc.encode() then split by token index, then enc.decode() back to string. Overlap: the last overlap_tokens tokens of chunk N become the first tokens of chunk N+1.

userStories.title: Google Drive connector
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to connect a Google Drive folder so RELAY indexes approved docs and syncs them into knowledge_chunks.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/google_drive.py implements GoogleDriveConnector(Connector)

sync(): reads GOOGLE_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID from source_connectors.config jsonb; decrypts credentials from source_connectors.encrypted_credentials; uses Google Drive API (google-api-python-client) to list files in the folder; for each file: export as plain text, compute content_hash, skip if hash unchanged, otherwise chunk with chunk_text() and call embed_chunks(); upsert source_documents row
disconnect(): sets source_connectors.disconnected_at = now()
purge(): deletes all knowledge_chunks and source_documents rows for this connector in this workspace
Connector credentials stored encrypted via relay.crypto.encrypt_token() / decrypt_token() — same pattern as workspace bot tokens
search() and citation() raise NotImplementedError with message 'Use retrieve() for search' (retrieval is centralized in US-006)
Unit tests in tests/test_google_drive_connector.py: mock Drive API, verify sync creates source_documents and knowledge_chunks rows, verify unchanged hash skips re-embed, verify purge deletes all rows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add google-api-python-client and google-auth to pyproject.toml. OAuth token for Drive is stored in source_connectors.encrypted_credentials as a JSON-serialized dict {access_token, refresh_token, token_uri, client_id, client_secret}. The OAuth connect flow (FastAPI route) is out of scope for this story — use a pre-configured token via GOOGLE_DRIVE_CREDENTIALS_JSON env var for v1.

userStories.title: GitHub connector
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to connect selected GitHub repositories so RELAY can cite issues, PRs, releases, and changelogs in customer responses.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/github.py implements GitHubConnector(Connector)

sync(): reads repo_list (list of 'owner/repo' strings) from source_connectors.config jsonb; uses GITHUB_TOKEN (decrypted from encrypted_credentials) with PyGitHub; for each repo: fetches open+closed issues (last 200), open+closed PRs (last 200), releases (last 50), selected markdown files from config.markdown_paths list; creates/upserts source_documents; chunks with chunk_text(); calls embed_chunks()
Each source_document.config jsonb stores: {type: issue|pr|release|markdown, status, labels: [], url, updated_at}
citation(chunk_row) -> dict: {title, url, status, labels, updated_at, stale: bool} where stale = (now - source_documents.last_synced_at) > 48h
disconnect() and purge() follow same pattern as GoogleDriveConnector
Unit tests in tests/test_github_connector.py: mock PyGitHub API, verify issues/PRs/releases sync creates correct rows, verify citation output format, verify stale flag logic
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add PyGitHub to pyproject.toml. GitHub token stored encrypted in source_connectors.encrypted_credentials. GITHUB_TOKEN env var is the fallback for dev. Markdown files: only files explicitly listed in config.markdown_paths are synced (e.g., CHANGELOG.md, docs/runbook.md). Do not recursively sync entire repos.

userStories.title: Tenant-safe semantic retrieval
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a retrieve() function that finds the most relevant knowledge_chunks for a query, always scoped to a single workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/connectors/retrieval.py implements async retrieve(workspace_id: uuid.UUID, query: str, top_k: int = 5, session: AsyncSession) -> list[RetrievedChunk]
RetrievedChunk dataclass: chunk_id (uuid.UUID), source_document_id (uuid.UUID | None), knowledge_entry_id (uuid.UUID | None), content (str), embedding_model (str), citation (dict)
Always filters by workspace_id before vector similarity: WHERE knowledge_chunks.workspace_id = :workspace_id ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT :top_k
Embeds query using embed_chunks() with a single-item list; uses the workspace's configured embedding model
Writes a retrieval_logs row for every call: query, sources_used (list of chunk_ids), retrieved_at
Unit tests in tests/test_retrieval.py: two workspaces with distinct chunks — verify workspace B chunks never appear in workspace A results; verify retrieval_log written
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: pgvector cosine distance operator is <=>. The SQL: SELECT id, content, source_document_id, knowledge_entry_id, embedding_model FROM knowledge_chunks WHERE workspace_id = :wid ORDER BY embedding <=> :qvec LIMIT :k. citation is built by joining source_documents and calling connector.citation(). For knowledge_entry chunks (knowledge_entry_id is not null), citation provider = 'relay_memory'.

userStories.title: Celery sync tasks and periodic scheduler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Celery tasks to trigger connector syncs and a Beat schedule to run them every 6 hours automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/worker/connector_tasks.py implements: sync_connector(workspace_id_str: str, connector_id_str: str) Celery task; sync_all_connectors() Celery task that queries all active (disconnected_at IS NULL) source_connectors and enqueues sync_connector per row
sync_connector: loads SourceConnector row, calls get_connector(connector_type).sync(workspace_id), updates last_synced_at and sync_status on success, sets sync_status='error' on exception
Celery Beat schedule in relay/worker/celery_app.py: sync_all_connectors runs every 6 hours (crontab(hour='*/6'))
Unit tests in tests/test_connector_tasks.py: mock connector sync(), verify last_synced_at updated, verify sync_status='error' on exception
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Celery Beat config uses beat_schedule dict in celery_app.py. Task args are strings (not UUIDs) for JSON serialization compatibility. Convert to uuid.UUID inside the task body. sync_connector catches all exceptions and sets sync_status='error' — it must not propagate exceptions (one failing connector must not block others).

userStories.title: App Home connector status section
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see connected sources and their sync status in the App Home so I know if retrieval data is fresh.
acceptanceCriteria: relay/slack/home.py gains a 'Connected Sources' section listing each active source_connectors row: connector type icon, last synced timestamp (human-readable, e.g. '3h ago'), sync_status badge (synced / syncing / error / never), staleness warning if last_synced_at > 24h ago
Section renders 'No sources connected. Use /relay settings to add a source.' if no active connectors exist
App Home refresh triggered on home_opened event (already wired) — no new wiring needed
Unit tests in tests/test_home.py (or existing home test): build_home() with one connector row returns blocks containing the connector type and sync status
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Build the blocks from the SourceConnector ORM rows. Do not call Slack API from inside the block builder — pass data in, return blocks. The icon per connector type: Google Drive = 📄, GitHub = 🐙, default = 🔌. staleness warning text: '⚠️ Last synced over 24h ago — retrieval may be stale.'
